title: Inverse design of chiral functional films by a robotic AI-guided system

paper_analysis.summary: This paper presents an AI-guided robotic chemistry platform that inverse-designs chiral stacked films with targeted circular dichroism (CD) and circularly polarized luminescence (CPL) across the visible spectrum. The system combines automated film preparation/characterization with machine-learning forward and reverse models, enabling discovery of heterostructured twisted bilayers with very large dissymmetry factors (gabs up to ~1.9) and demonstration in CP-selective color filters and switchable CPL films.

figures.description: Overall workflow of the AI-Chemist: robotic preparation/characterization of twisted bilayer chiral films, machine-learning forward model, and inverse design for targeted chiroptical properties and applications.
Comparison of homo- vs heterostructured bilayer films and how CD peak position, bandwidth, and intensity depend on thickness, strain, dye absorption, and absorbance.
Map of the robotic work area and photographs of the AI-chemist performing material selection, dyeing, stretching, stacking, and chiroptical characterization.
Two-round clustering and prescreening of film parameters using LD and transmittance to reduce the design space.
Reverse-model inverse design examples, including predicted vs measured CD spectra and parameter sets for target gabs values at selected wavelengths.
Maps of available inverse-design solutions for target gabs values at different wavelengths and dyes, plus wavelength dependence of maximum achievable gabs.
Inverse-designed CP-selective color filters for multiplex laser display, including predicted vs measured CD spectra, optical images under RCP/LCP, and CIE1931 gamut coverage.
Inverse design of chiral films for CPL from perovskite quantum dot emission, including target-function definition, recommended parameters, experimental CPPL, and off-resonance tuning of CPL spectra.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw experimental measurements collected by the robotic platform and instruments, including UV–vis spectra for transmittance/absorption, LD spectra, CD spectra, CPPL spectra, TEM images of PQDs, XRD patterns of PQD/PVA films, and SEM confirmation of film thickness uniformity. The robot also performs optical imaging of color-filter outputs under RCP/LCP illumination.
paper_analysis.processed_plot_details: Many figures plot processed or derived quantities: LD and T0 histograms used for clustering, CD/gabs spectra sampled every 5 nm from 200–800 nm, model-predicted vs measured spectra, parameter-space maps of inverse-design solutions, CIE1931 chromaticity regions, and CPPL spectra derived from combining CD response with emission-weighted target functions.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The provided text does not include a dedicated data availability statement. It does state that the AI-Chemist fabricated 1493 films for the forward model, and that additional validation experiments were performed for inverse design, but it does not specify repository links, file formats, or whether raw/source data and code are deposited.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data in the paper are experimental instrument outputs: UV–vis spectra, LD spectra, CD spectra, CPPL spectra, TEM micrographs, XRD patterns, SEM images, and optical photographs. The text does not specify file formats, folder structure, or whether raw files are publicly provided.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include spectral embedded descriptors built from absorption values every 5 nm from 200 to 800 nm plus structural/process parameters (twist angle, thickness, strain, greyscale), clustered LD/T0 statistics for prescreening, and target-function outputs for inverse design. The paper also reports model metrics (MAE, RMSE, R2) and derived gabs/glum spectra.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly implied: predicted vs measured spectra, histograms, clustering results, parameter maps, chromaticity coordinates, and CPPL outputs. However, the text does not explicitly say that per-figure source data files are deposited, only that the figures are based on experimental and model-generated datasets.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: The text mentions machine-learning models and training details, but no explicit analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repository are described in the provided excerpt.
dataset_characterization.replot_reason: The paper provides enough methodological detail to understand how plots were generated, but the excerpt does not specify access to raw data tables, source-data files, or scripts. Therefore the data are not confirmed to be directly replot-ready from the paper text alone.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is semantically hybrid: it contains raw experimental spectra/images, processed descriptors for ML, and derived figure data for model validation and inverse-design maps. The core training dataset consists of 1493 experimentally fabricated films with measured CD spectra and associated structure/process parameters. The reverse-design examples are generated by the model and then experimentally verified, but the excerpt does not distinguish any public raw-vs-source-data packaging.
